turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="sky" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#bfe8ff"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#eaf7ff"/>
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="ground" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#a9d18e"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#6fbe67"/>
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- Background -->
  <rect x="0" y="0" width="512" height="512" fill="url(#sky)"/>

  <!-- Sun -->
  <circle cx="395" cy="115" r="42" fill="#ffd36a"/>
  <g stroke="#ffd36a" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round" opacity="0.9">
    <line x1="395" y1="58" x2="395" y2="28"/>
    <line x1="452" y1="115" x2="482" y2="115"/>
    <line x1="395" y1="172" x2="395" y2="202"/>
    <line x1="338" y1="115" x2="308" y2="115"/>
    <line x1="437" y1="72" x2="459" y2="50"/>
    <line x1="353" y1="72" x2="331" y2="50"/>
    <line x1="353" y1="158" x2="331" y2="180"/>
    <line x1="437" y1="158" x2="459" y2="180"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Clouds -->
  <g fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.95">
    <ellipse cx="130" cy="125" rx="55" ry="28"/>
    <ellipse cx="165" cy="118" rx="45" ry="26"/>
    <ellipse cx="105" cy="130" rx="40" ry="24"/>
    <ellipse cx="235" cy="175" rx="52" ry="26"/>
    <ellipse cx="270" cy="168" rx="42" ry="24"/>
    <ellipse cx="215" cy="182" rx="38" ry="22"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Bird silhouettes (freedom/flight) -->
  <g fill="#2f3a4a" opacity="0.9">
    <path d="M255 210 C245 196 232 191 218 195 C232 186 245 185 255 195 C265 185 278 186 292 195 C278 191 265 196 255 210 Z"/>
    <path d="M320 245 C312 233 301 229 289 232 C301 225 312 224 320 232 C328 224 339 225 351 232 C339 229 328 233 320 245 Z" opacity="0.75"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Ground -->
  <path d="M0 360 C90 330 170 365 256 355 C350 344 420 320 512 345 L512 512 L0 512 Z" fill="url(#ground)"/>

  <!-- Open door / gateway -->
  <g transform="translate(120,110)">
    <!-- Door frame -->
    <rect x="0" y="110" width="272" height="300" rx="16" fill="#c7b49a" stroke="#8a735a" stroke-width="6"/>

    <!-- Left door panel (closed side) -->
    <path d="M40 140 L40 400 L140 430 L140 140 Z" fill="#d9c6a8" stroke="#8a735a" stroke-width="6"/>
    <!-- Right door panel (open side, shifted) -->
    <path d="M232 140 L232 400 L170 430 L170 140 Z" fill="#d9c6a8" stroke="#8a735a" stroke-width="6"/>

    <!-- Gap of freedom (bright opening) -->
    <rect x="140" y="150" width="36" height="260" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.55"/>

    <!-- Door handle (no text) -->
    <circle cx="110" cy="285" r="10" fill="#6b4f3c"/>
    <rect x="100" y="275" width="20" height="20" rx="10" fill="#6b4f3c" opacity="0.35"/>

    <!-- Arch opening accent -->
    <path d="M64 210 C78 160 118 135 136 135 C160 135 204 164 206 210 L206 220 C190 186 160 170 136 170 C115 170 88 186 70 220 Z" fill="#ffffff" opacity="0.25"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Light rays from opening -->
  <g opacity="0.35">
    <path d="M256 190 L256 360" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="10" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M240 205 L235 365" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M272 205 L277 365" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M224 215 L210 370" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <path d="M288 215 L302 370" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  </g>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating a visual representation**

I’m working on a guess-the-word game where I need to visually represent the concept of "freedom" without using any text. I think of imagery like an open sky, a bird, an open door, or an unlocked lock. Drawing an open padlock could symbolize freedom, but maybe a more abstract representation would be better, like an open door with rising bird silhouettes against a cloudy sky. I'll ensure it’s created in SVG format, maintaining simplicity.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}
